content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Patched `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs` additively to close both pending failure-reconciliation defects. Historical backfills now trigger a same-pass repair sweep that recomputes and rewrites later recorded failed attempts on the same bead so `BeadFailed` journal details and repaired lineage outcome details no longer retain stale `attempt=` markers. Retry ordering now uses an explicit attempt identity keyed by `project_id`, `started_at`, and a deterministic `run_id` tiebreaker instead of derived `Ord` on opaque run IDs, so retry numbering follows actual chronology and ignores failures from other projects. I also added focused regressions for chronological ordering, project scoping, and later-attempt marker repair after historical backfill.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Reworked failure-attempt identity/order logic in `failure_reconciliation.rs` so retry numbering is derived from explicit chronological attempt keys scoped to the current project instead of derived ordering on `run_id` strings.
2. [Completed] Added a historical-backfill repair pass that reloads recorded failed attempts after stale replay repair, recomputes canonical ordinals, and repairs any later failed lineage/journal records whose `attempt=` markers are now stale.
3. [Completed] Preserved existing provenance repair behavior while extending recorded failure provenance to carry the fields needed for same-pass journal and lineage rewrites during backfill reconciliation.
4. [Completed] Added focused regression coverage for chronological retry ordering, cross-project retry isolation, and repairing later failed attempt markers immediately after historical backfill, then reran the Rust validation suite.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `cargo test failure_reconciliation -- --nocapture` passed; all 21 failure-reconciliation tests succeeded, including the new chronology/project-scope/backfill-repair regressions.
- `cargo test` passed on the full suite.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passed.
- `cargo clippy --features test-stub -- -D warnings` passed.
- `nix build` was launched and progressed into `/nix/store/8ppxgfq2vyvbaj66f8byqjncplcxjikb-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0.drv`, but no final exit status was observed in-session.

## Outstanding Risks

- `nix build` was launched on the updated tree and remained in the active derivation build stage without returning a final success/failure exit in-session, so the authoritative Nix gate is still unresolved.
- Pre-existing live orchestration-state edits under `.ralph-burning/projects/9ni854-1/` were left untouched and remain out of scope.
